recommendations.reason: TOP PICK. Proposal planned 2026-05-14 (one day before this exploration). Pain ('tasks.md checkbox drift, lost reviewer trust') is the freshest, most concrete user-facing UX pain in the signal field. S effort, all infra exists, ~250 LOC. Recommend execution rather than inventing a new proposal.

Operator-side complement to task-status-renderer. One-command 'why is this feature stuck?' that aggregates locks + work-queue + approvals + artifacts + worktree state. Directly applies the attention-bottleneck framing the factory-missions work just codified.

Collapses the April 3-step visualization stack (SSE + TUI + web dashboard + Tauri) into one minimal HTML page served by FastAPI. Strategically supersedes add-agent-state-stream + add-agent-state-tui + add-coordinator-web-dashboard as a NEAR-TERM move; those remain follow-ons.
New /find-skill fuzzy-search across the 80+ skill catalog. Worth doing but lower urgency than the two top picks.
Structured pre-compact handoff renderer. Composes with session-bootstrap (actively iterated). Promote only if /why-am-i-blocked ships and compact-quality pain re-emerges.
Superseded as a near-term move by add-coordinator-glance-page (polling instead of streaming). Re-rank to recommended if glance-page polling proves too chatty.
Per-repo worktree registries aggregated to coordinator. Needed for cross-repo global view in the user's multi-window ghostty workflow.
Textual TUI subscribing to the SSE stream. Fits ghostty workflow natively (runs in a tab/window). Python-native, no new stack. Validates data model before any desktop/web investment.
Deferred until TUI validates which views matter. Zero-new-runtime implementation once stream lands.
Complements real-time UIs with historical analytics over existing OTEL metrics. Can ship independently as a parallel package.
Introduces Rust/JS toolchain with no reuse elsewhere. Revisit only if web dashboard proves insufficient. Thin wrapper once web app exists.
Platform-locked Swift stack with zero repo reuse. A Tauri tray icon covers most of the value cross-platform.
Previous top pick; carried forward. Still relevant (the web dashboard benefits from tunnel access) but orthogonal to the current visualization focus.
Carried forward; complementary to tunnel. Not on the critical path for visualization.
Carried forward (4th cycle). Big bet; orthogonal to visualization focus.
Carried forward. Blocked on tunnel setup.
Carried forward. CORS work will matter once the web dashboard lands.
Carried forward; blocked on tunnel.
Carried forward; blocked on single-env DNS.
